content:
Now let me examine the Data sheet more carefully to understand the structure for the lookup formulas:

```json
{
  "analysis": "I can see the Task sheet structure clearly. There are three sections of yellow cells to fill:
1. H12:L17 - Exports data (series code BXGS_BP6.A)
2. H19:L24 - Imports data (series code BMGS_BP6.A)
3. H26:L31 - GDP data (series code NGDPD.A)

Each section has 6 countries (UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Oman, Saudi Arabia) and years 2019-2023 (columns H-L). The blue conditions are in column D (series codes) and row 10 (years). I need to examine the Data sheet structure to understand how to construct the INDEX&MATCH formulas.",
  "plan": "I'll examine the Data sheet structure more carefully to understand: (1) How the data is organized, (2) Which rows contain the series codes and years, (3) The exact layout to construct proper INDEX&MATCH formulas. Then I'll create formulas that look up values based on both series code and year.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 << 'EOF'
from openpyxl import load_workbook

file_path = 'gdp.xlsx'
wb = load_workbook(file_path, data_only=False)
data_ws = wb['Data']

print("=== DATA SHEET FULL STRUCTURE ===")
print("\nFirst 10 rows:")
for row_idx in range(1, 11):
    row_data = []
    for col_idx in range(1, 16):
        cell = data_ws.cell(row_idx, col_idx)
        row_data.append(str(cell.value)[:20] if cell.value else '')
    print(f"Row {row_idx}: {row_data}")

print("\nRows 20-40:")
for row_idx in range(20, 41):
    row_data = []
    for col_idx in range(1, 16):
        cell = data_ws.cell(row_idx, col_idx)
        row_data.append(str(cell.value)[:20] if cell.value else '')
    print(f"Row {row_idx}: {row_data}")

EOF
",
      "duration": 2.0
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": false
}
```
